assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and internet access.
No platform account, credentials, or product resources exist yet.

developer_goal: Create a Better Stack account and monitor a public HTTP endpoint.

first_success.outcome: A hosted HTTP monitor checks the supplied URL and reports its current status.
first_success.observable_signal: The monitor detail page shows a completed check and an Up status for the endpoint.

route_selection.rule: Prefer a hosted or direct API route over local installation, and prefer documented cURL when it reaches the same first success.

candidates.name: Uptime dashboard: first HTTP monitor
candidates.reason: The hosted dashboard path creates a working HTTP monitor without installing an agent or writing code.

candidates.name: Heartbeat monitor
candidates.reason: A heartbeat requires an external job to call Better Stack; an HTTP monitor can check an existing URL directly.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to a Better Stack account and open Uptime.
Choose to create a monitor and select HTTP monitoring.
Enter the endpoint URL and save the monitor.
required_path.observable_result: The monitor is listed and checks begin.

required_path.action: Open the monitor and confirm the first completed check.

prerequisites.description: A publicly reachable HTTP or HTTPS URL to monitor.

external_gates.description: The monitored URL must be reachable from Better Stack.

platform_outcomes.description: A hosted HTTP monitor checks the supplied URL and reports its current status.

excluded.reason: These occur after the first observable end-to-end result.

uncertainties.question: The docs do not fully expose every fresh-account signup or verification screen.
uncertainties.impact: The required path may differ for some fresh accounts.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account walkthrough through the authenticated first-success state.
